titleStmt.title: SII 9.60: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 60. (A.R. No. 512 of 1915.) ON A SLAB LYING IN A FIELD NEAR THE RUINED TEMPLE OF ISVARA AT DODDIMAKALA, ADONI TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: The record is dated Śaka 852, Khara, Phālguṇa, śuddha pañchamī, Śukravāra corresponding to A.D. 931 February 25, Friday. It refers itself to the reign of the Rāshṭrakūṭa king Gōyindara-Ballaha (i.e. Gōvinda IV). His Sāmanta named Kannara is said to have been ruling over Sindavāḍi one-thousand district. A certain Kuruḷa-Kāmaseṭṭi is said to have made a gift of 50 mattars of land to the temple of Kāmēśvara and distributed them among the temple-servants such as stone-masons, the astrologer, the singer of hymns and others.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . sakanṛpakālātītasaṁvatsara
sataṅgaḷeṇṭunūṟayvatteraḍaneya kharamemba
saṁvatsarada phālguṇamāsa śuddhapañcami śukravāraṁ
śrīgōyindaraballahanāśamudraparyya
nta śukhadināḷe tatpādapadmōpajīvitanivāsi samadhigatapañca
mahāśabda mahāsāmanta kannaraṁ sindavāḍisāyiramu
manuttarōttaraṁ sukhadināḷe muṟunuyyaṁ jñānaśivabhaṭārarddēvabhōgamāḷe
Āycaṇa gāvuṇḍanāge śrīmatvaśyakulatilakaṁ baḷagāramahōda
dhi kuruḷakāmaseṭṭiya kāmēsvarada dharmaśāsanada dattiyaṁ
Ereya keyya Irppattaydu kisukāḍu Irppattaydu Anturāja
māna Āyvattu mattaradaṟoḷage kāsigaṁge Ere Āṟu mattar
kisukāḍu Āṟumattarantu koṇḍōjaṅge pannirmmattaṟpaṟekā
ṟaṅge Ere pannormmattarhāḍuvoṅge kisukāḍāṟu mattar
Īśānaśivaṁge Ere pannirmmatta jōtiṣabha cāvuṇḍayyaṅge
nālku mattartōṇṭigaḷge nālku mattarnnivēdyakke nellamaḍi Eraḍu ke
ṟege maḍi Eraḍu nāḻgāmuṇḍana Oḍtaḻivana Āycaṇana puddhana
nāḍadhyakṣade māḍida śāsana Idanaḻidoṁ vāraṇāsiya
karu kanranaḻidoṁ . maṅgaḷa .
Digital edition of SII 9.60 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
